elements.title: Atlas
elements.contents: ... that Atlas (pictured) was the last of her kind to be built in the United States, and later became a canning company's flagship?

elements.title: Jacob Huesman
elements.contents: ... that Jacob Huesman joined the New York Giants as a fullback despite never having played the position before?

elements.title: January 1926 Liechtenstein general election
elements.contents: ... that a candidate in the January 1926 Liechtenstein general election was reported to the police for allegedly supporting republican views?

elements.title: North Fork Snoqualmie River
elements.contents: ... that a proposed dam on the North Fork Snoqualmie River may have been an attempt to gain political leverage over Seattle?

elements.title: Ceciwa Khonje
elements.contents: ... that exiled Ceciwa Khonje's role in achieving Malawi's independence was "rubbed out of history"?

elements.title: Lebanon uses 15 different legal frameworks and religious courts
elements.contents: ... that Lebanon uses 15 different legal frameworks and religious courts to represent the country's 18 officially recognized sects, as opposed to a common or unified civil personal status law?

elements.title: S. Sudjojono
elements.contents: ... that Indonesian painter S. Sudjojono left the Communist Party of Indonesia after it deemed him to be too immoral?

elements.title: incineration of more than 516,000 cans of soup
elements.contents: ... that the US government contracted for the incineration of more than 516,000 cans of soup?

elements.title: was saved by a whale's tail
elements.contents: ... that an overshooting train in Rotterdam was saved by a whale's tail?
